assumptions_to_verify: The tests actually assert the distinct families and conservative rules.
contradictions_checked: Claude claimed tests were not run, but evidence receipts confirm they were run and passed.
critical_review.strongest_objection: Claude noted a mismatch in the TDD document regarding a test name in a regression command, but the actual tests exist and pass.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the tests did not actually cover the distinct family requirement or if the conservative rules were not enforced.

claims: The tdd plan is sound, complete, public-boundary, and grill-resolved
All planned tests are realized as real non-vacuous tests in the working tree
Tests are deterministic and do not invoke live Codex/Cursor
Non-goals are respected: no calibrated weighting, no agentic mislabel, conservative rules unchanged
confidence_criteria: All 5 planned tests present and named consistently with the plan body: yes
Tests hit the public boundary where required: yes (3 via run_dual_agent_workflow tool)
Tests non-vacuous (assert distinct families, blocking, degraded-not-accept): yes
Fixtures exist and prevent live calls: yes
Tests actually executed green: NOT verified (pytest approval blocked)
Regression commands runnable verbatim: NO (line 61 names nonexistent test)
outcome.confidence_rationale: High-confidence on test-plan soundness and realization: every planned test was read and verified non-vacuous and public-boundary, fixtures exist, and grill findings are resolved. Held below 0.9 because pytest was not executed (approval blocked) so GREEN is inspection-only, handoff byte-hashes were not re-verified, and there is a real (though non-blocking) regression-command naming defect.

assumptions_to_verify: Suite actually passes when pytest runs (currently inspection-only)
Default codex_runner fake injection holds for every test path so CI never hits live Codex
Handoff artifact bytes match the declared sha256 hashes
contradictions_checked: TDD section-3 test name vs regression-command name: MISMATCH found (cmd name is stale; real test exists under section name)
Adapter sets reviewer_assurance tool_backed_primary vs panel asserts assurance_grade agentic: consistent across the two field layers, both gated on command_executions
Non-goal 'no calibrated weighting' vs presence of low_confidence_threshold: consistent — default 0.0 (off), opt-in only
Non-goal 'no agentic mislabel' vs codex labeled agentic: truthful — gated on command_executions in the JSONL
missing_evidence: Empirical pytest run confirming all 5 tests pass (GREEN) — Bash/pytest approval not granted in this env
Re-computed sha256 of handoff planning artifacts vs packet hashes — shasum blocked
Confirmation that the panel-level assurance_grade 'agentic' mapping is faithful to adapter reviewer_assurance under all command_execution states
critical_review.strongest_objection: The focused regression command in tdd.md:61 references a test name that does not exist (test_run_dual_agent_workflow_records_two_independent_reviewers); the actual public-boundary test is test_workflow_exposes_independent_reviewer_results_and_dual_writes_events. Running that exact command verbatim errors at collection.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A pytest run showing any of the 5 tests fail or pass vacuously, evidence that a test secretly invokes live Codex/Cursor, or discovery that a planned acceptance dimension (distinct family, truthful assurance, conservative-block parity, honest degrade) is unasserted.

objections: tdd.md:61 focused regression command names test_run_dual_agent_workflow_records_two_independent_reviewers which does not exist; the real test is test_workflow_exposes_independent_reviewer_results_and_dual_writes_events (line 2776). That exact command errors at collection, but coverage is preserved by the file-level and full-suite commands (lines 62-63). REVISE-grade nit, not blocking.
Residual: pytest not run (Bash approval blocked); GREEN verified by inspection only and handoff byte-hashes not re-verified.

specialists.name: lead-gate-reviewer
specialists.objection: tdd.md:61 regression command references nonexistent test name; coverage intact via broader commands

outcome.summary: tdd_review ACCEPT: all 5 planned tests (P1-P5) realized as real non-vacuous tests; 3 hit the public boundary via run_dual_agent_workflow, 2 cover the registry/adapter agentic bar (tool+read-only sandbox+hash). Fake runners injected by default so no test calls live Codex; grill 3/3 resolved; non-goals respected (no weighting, no agentic mislabel, conservative rules unchanged). One non-blocking artifact defect: tdd.md:61 focused regression command names a nonexistent test.
